name: Herero
notes: Herero (Otjiherero), a Bantu language of Namibia and Botswana. INPUT CONTRACT: the standard Latin Otjiherero orthography (the practical missionary-derived spelling used in Namibian publications and schools) is assumed; text is expected to be already normalised. KEY FEATURES: (1) DIGRAPHS/TRIGRAPHS: <tj> = /tʃ/ (as in Otjiherero), <ny> = /ɲ/, and the prenasalised series <mb> = /ᵐb/, <nd> = /ⁿd/, <ndj> = /ⁿdʒ/, <ng> = /ᵑɡ/, with labialised <ngw> = /ᵑɡʷ/ and <mw> = /ᵐw/. (2) VOWEL LENGTH is contrastive and IS written, by doubling: <aa ee ii oo uu> = /ɑː ɛː iː ɔː uː/. (3) FIVE VOWEL QUALITIES /i ɛ ɑ ɔ u/; <u> is also read /w/ in glide position. (4) <z> = /ð/ (voiced interdental fricative). (5) /f s l/ occur only in loanwords. (6) DENTAL SERIES NOT RECOVERABLE: Otjiherero contrasts dental /t̪ n̪ ⁿd̪/ with alveolar /t n ⁿd/, and has a voiceless interdental /θ/; the practical orthography writes the dentals with the same letters <t n nd s> as the alveolars (only scholarly transcriptions add sub-diacritics, e.g. ṱ ṋ nḓ ṣ), so dentality is NOT distinguished here — the alveolar value is emitted. (7) TONE: Otjiherero is tonal, but TONE IS NOT WRITTEN in the standard orthography (only in linguistic transcriptions, which mark high/low/mid/rising/falling with diacritics), so it is NOT recoverable from ordinary text and is not emitted.

sources.author: Ager, S.
sources.title: Herero language, alphabet and pronunciation
sources.notes: Letter-to-IPA chart for the Herero alphabet, including digraphs (mb, mw, nd, ndj, ng, ngw, ny, tj), doubled long vowels, and the tone-marking diacritics used in transcription.

sources.author: Möhlig, W.J.G., Marten, L. & Kavari, J.U.
sources.title: A Grammatical Sketch of Herero (Otjiherero)
sources.notes: Reference grammar; source of the consonant inventory (dental vs alveolar series, prenasalised stops, /θ ð/) and the note that /f s l/ occur only in loanwords.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Herero language — Phonology
sources.notes: Consonant and vowel tables: /m n̪ n ɲ/, /p t̪ t tʃ k/, /ᵐb ⁿd̪ ⁿd ⁿdʒ ᵑɡ/, /θ ð v h/, /r w j/, five vowel qualities with contrastive length.

ancestors.notes: Atlantic-Congo — the classification node this language sits under.
